title: cassine sphaerophylla
intro: Cassine sphaerophylla, also known as the Cassine or Round-leaved Cassine, is a plant species belonging to the Celastraceae family. It is a small, deciduous shrub native to South Africa, with a round, leathery leaf and small, greenish-yellow flowers. Cassine sphaerophylla has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which have been found to aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, coughs, and skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
